sources: Official municipal transit sites (RATP, BVG, ATM Milano, TfL, IETT, EGO, etc.)

Platform coverage pages: BlaBlaCar, Lime, Tier, Dott, Voi, Bolt, Uber, Free Now, Heetch, Share Now, Free2move, Vélib', Bicing, BiciMAD, Swapfiets, Donkey Republic, MOL Bubi, Nextbike, Veturilo, JOB, MOBIKE, MARTI, BiTaksi, BinBin, İSPARK

ridesharing.notes: BlaBlaCar active for intercity routes; domestic carpooling culture is modest compared to DE/FR.
BlaBlaCar covers long-distance; Klaxit/Karos used for commuter pooling.
BlaBlaCar popular for Sofia–Plovdiv–Varna; intercity buses remain dominant.
Strong BlaBlaCar usage; competes with cheap RegioJet/FlixBus.
Germany is BlaBlaCar's largest market after France; FlixBus (same parent) is the main alternative.
GoMore (Danish origin) is the leading peer-to-peer carpool + car rental platform.
Formal ridesharing thin; most intercity travel uses Lux Express buses.
Huge BlaBlaCar market for Madrid–Barcelona–Valencia corridors.
Carpooling is informal; national rail and OnniBus dominate.
BlaBlaCar's home market; massive coverage on every route.
Limited formal ridesharing; KTEL buses and ferries dominate.
Coastal summer demand pushes BlaBlaCar listings up; FlixBus strong too.
Oszkár is the local Hungarian carpool platform alongside BlaBlaCar.
Small market; Irish Rail and Bus Éireann remain default.
BlaBlaCar solid, especially Milan–Rome–Naples; Trenitalia/Italo competitive on price.
Distances short; NS trains and cycling make ridesharing niche.
Traditional carpooling rare; peer-to-peer car rental via Nabobil/Hyre common.
Very popular; BlaBlaCar competes head-to-head with FlixBus on intercity.
BlaBlaCar modest; Rede Expressos bus network dominates.
GoMore active for long trips; Skjutsgruppen is a non-profit social ridesharing network.
BlaBlaCar relaunched in Turkiye; intercity buses (Kamil Koç, Metro Turizm) still dominate.

berlin.tips: BVG ticket already covers most distances — use bikes for short hops, not whole days.

munich.priceRange: Scooter ~1€ unlock + 0.25€/min; MVG Rad first 30 min free for MVG subscribers
munich.tips: MVG Rad is the cheapest bike option if you have an MVG day ticket.

hamburg.priceRange: StadtRAD first 30 min free, then 0.10€/min (cap ~15€/day); MOIA pooled ride ~5–10€
hamburg.tips: MOIA pooled electric shuttles are a cheap night option across central Hamburg.

paris.priceRange: Vélib' day pass ~5€ (first 30 min free each ride); eBike ~1€ + 0.25€/min
paris.tips: Free-floating e-scooters were banned in Paris from 2023 — only eBikes remain. Vélib' day pass is the best value.

lyon.priceRange: Vélo'v 24h ticket ~1.80€; first 30 min free per ride
lyon.tips: Vélo'v is superb value and integrates with TCL transit app.

nice.priceRange: Vélo Bleu day pass ~1.50€; first 30 min free
nice.tips: Promenade des Anglais bike path is the main reason to grab a Vélo Bleu.

rome.priceRange: Scooter ~1€ unlock + 0.22€/min; Free Now taxi start ~4€
rome.tips: Standard UberX is not available in Rome — use Free Now or itTaxi for regular taxis.

milan.tips: BikeMi is cheaper than scooters for tourist loops through the Centro Storico.

florence.tips: Historic centre is a ZTL (restricted traffic zone) — walking or e-bikes beat scooters.

madrid.priceRange: BiciMAD single ride ~0.60€ first 30 min; scooter ~1€ unlock + 0.20€/min
madrid.tips: Cabify is often cheaper than Uber in Madrid; BiciMAD is electric-assist everywhere.

barcelona.priceRange: Donkey Republic day pass ~15€; scooter services restricted
barcelona.tips: Bicing requires a local NIE/registration — tourists should use Donkey Republic instead. Most free-floating scooter brands pulled out in 2023.

seville.priceRange: Sevici week pass ~13€; first 30 min free
seville.tips: Sevici has excellent docking station coverage — better than app scooters for all-day touring.

amsterdam.tips: Free-floating e-scooters are banned; rent a regular city bike for 24h — it is the only sensible way to move.

rotterdam.tips: OV-fiets at the train station is by far the cheapest option if you have an OV-chipkaart.

brussels.tips: Villo! is cheap but has hills — e-scooters win for the upper town.

ghent.tips: Ghent is compact and flat — one regular city bike covers the whole trip.

vienna.tips: WienMobil app bundles transit, bikes and scooters into one ticket.

zurich.priceRange: PubliBike ~3 CHF first 30 min; scooter ~0.30 CHF/min
zurich.tips: Züri rollt lends regular bikes for free with a deposit — unbeatable for day trips.

prague.priceRange: Rekola ~60 CZK/30 min; scooter ~25 CZK unlock + 4 CZK/min
prague.tips: Liftago is the local taxi app — often cheaper than Uber and drivers speak Czech.

warsaw.priceRange: Veturilo first 20 min free with 10 PLN registration; scooter ~3 PLN unlock + 0.6 PLN/min
warsaw.tips: Veturilo is one of Europe's best-value bike-shares — register once, reuse for years.

krakow.tips: Old Town is a pedestrian zone — park scooters outside the Planty ring.

budapest.priceRange: MOL Bubi day pass ~500 HUF; scooter ~200 HUF unlock + 70 HUF/min
budapest.tips: Uber is not available in Budapest — Bolt and Főtaxi cover ride-hailing.

lisbon.tips: Hills are brutal — always grab an e-bike (GIRA has both classic and electric).

porto.tips: No municipal bike-share yet; Uber/Bolt fares are the cheapest in Western Europe.

athens.tips: Uber in Athens only dispatches licensed taxis (UberTaxi). Bolt/FreeNow are the main apps.

thessaloniki.tips: Flat seafront promenade makes scooters genuinely fun here.

stockholm.priceRange: Scooter ~10 SEK unlock + 3 SEK/min; day pass options ~79 SEK
stockholm.tips: Voi day pass is worth it for islands hopping in summer.

copenhagen.priceRange: Donkey Republic day ~10€; scooter ~10 DKK unlock + 2.5 DKK/min
copenhagen.tips: Uber is not available in Denmark. Bike infrastructure is world-class — always rent one.

helsinki.priceRange: HSL bike day pass ~5€; scooter ~1€ unlock + 0.25€/min
helsinki.tips: HSL bikes only run May–October; after that, grab a scooter or walk.

dublin.tips: E-scooters legalised in 2024 but public rental schemes are still limited — stick with Dublinbikes.

istanbul.priceRange: Scooter ~10 TL unlock + 2.5 TL/min; BiTaksi is metered standard taxi
istanbul.tips: Traffic and hills make scooters risky — use ferries + metro. MARTI is the biggest scooter app.

ankara.tips: EGO metro + Ankaray trains cover the core — scooters mostly for Kızılay nightlife.

izmir.priceRange: BİSİM ~7 TL/hour with İzmirim Kart; scooter ~10 TL unlock + 2.5 TL/min
izmir.tips: Seafront Kordon promenade is perfect for BİSİM — flat and dedicated bike path.

oslo.priceRange: Bysykkel day pass ~49 NOK; scooter ~10 NOK unlock + 3 NOK/min
oslo.tips: Bysykkel season runs April–November; outside that only scooters/transit.

zagreb.tips: Uber and Bolt are reliably cheap — often beat taxis by 30–40%.

sofia.tips: Uber is not in Bulgaria — Bolt is the default ride-hailing app.

tallinn.tips: Bolt is Estonian — whole mobility stack (ride, scooter, car, food) lives in one app here.
